summary: CORS configuration is insecure/invalid for credentialed requests, proxy integration URI will break root route, and API mapping references the wrong stage field.

issues.description: CORS allow_origins includes wildcard patterns and untrusted domains while allow_credentials=true. HTTP API CORS does not support wildcard subdomains, and credentialed CORS with broad origins creates a cookie-leak risk.
issues.current_code:
```hcl
  cors_configuration {
    allow_origins     = [
      "https://*.0xhoneyjar.xyz",
      "https://0xhoneyjar.xyz",
      "https://*.vercel.app",
    ]
    allow_methods     = ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS", "PATCH"]
    allow_headers     = ["*"]
    expose_headers    = ["*"]
    allow_credentials = true
    max_age           = 86400
  }
```
issues.fixed_code:
```hcl
locals {
  # Explicit allowlist required for credentialed CORS (no wildcards).
  auth_proxy_allowed_origins = [
    "https://0xhoneyjar.xyz",
    "https://app.0xhoneyjar.xyz",
    "https://dashboard.0xhoneyjar.xyz",
    "https://community.0xhoneyjar.xyz",
  ]
}

  cors_configuration {
    allow_origins     = local.auth_proxy_allowed_origins
    allow_methods     = ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS", "PATCH"]
    allow_headers     = ["Authorization", "Content-Type", "X-Requested-With", "Accept", "Origin"]
    expose_headers    = ["Content-Length", "Content-Type"]
    allow_credentials = true
    max_age           = 86400
  }
```
issues.explanation: HTTP API CORS does not accept wildcard subdomains and browsers disallow credentialed CORS with wildcard origins. An explicit allowlist prevents credential leakage to arbitrary origins and ensures CORS config applies reliably.

issues.description: integration_uri uses a {proxy} path parameter that does not exist on the root route (ANY /), causing failures or forwarding to a literal {proxy} path. HTTP API proxy integrations automatically append the request path.
issues.explanation: Using {proxy} in the integration URI breaks the root route because there is no path parameter to substitute. For HTTP API proxy integrations, the request path and query string are automatically forwarded when the base URI is provided.

issues.description: API mapping stage uses the stage ID instead of the stage name. The API mapping expects the stage name and can fail or map incorrectly.
issues.current_code:
```hcl
resource "aws_apigatewayv2_api_mapping" "auth" {
  api_id      = aws_apigatewayv2_api.auth_proxy.id
  domain_name = aws_apigatewayv2_domain_name.auth.id
  stage       = aws_apigatewayv2_stage.default.id
}
```
issues.fixed_code:
```hcl
resource "aws_apigatewayv2_api_mapping" "auth" {
  api_id      = aws_apigatewayv2_api.auth_proxy.id
  domain_name = aws_apigatewayv2_domain_name.auth.id
  stage       = aws_apigatewayv2_stage.default.name
}
```
issues.explanation: The API mapping requires the stage name (e.g., "$default"), not the stage ID. Using the name ensures correct custom domain mapping.
